extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won after VAN AVERMAET Greg launched 300m too early on a gradient that eased, allowing Sagan to time his sprint perfectly from fifth wheel.
tactical_patterns: Sky drove tempo final 10km specifically to drop GAVIRIA Fernando, effectively handing SAGAN the sprint by eliminating his fastest pure sprinter rival.
VAN AVERMAET Greg's attack mistimed for gradient profile: launched when steep but finish eased final 500m, allowing chasers to carry speed.
GILBERT Philippe bridged to intermediate bonus sprint after breakaway caught 100m prior, revealing form inconsistent with pre-race GC dismissal.
SKUJIŅŠ Toms dominated breakaway work but polka dot tie goes to him via last climb precedence rule over CHAVANEL Sylvain.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: Director called this his best form of the tour through five stages, showing progressive improvement trajectory rather than peak-and-fade.
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Dropped on Sky's 10km tempo effort before final climb, exposing climbing limitation that makes him exploitable on rolling sprint stages.
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: positive: Took 2-second bonus at intermediate sprint and animated finale, positioning aggressively for yellow jersey time gaps despite non-GC rider status.
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: Contested both intermediate bonus sprint and finale after bridging from caught breakaway, showing unexpected late-race punch for GC ambitions.
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: neutral: Named as sprint threat to watch but invisible in finale, suggesting form or positioning issues on technical punchy finishes.
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: Abandoned sick before stage start, eliminating green jersey defending threat and opening competition for SAGAN exploitation.
rider_signals.SKUJIŅŠ Toms: positive: Strongest breakaway rider, frustrated by CHAVANEL tactical sitting but still secured polka dots, signaling climbing domestique utility or breakaway threat.
